---
title: |
  Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML<br>Unit 11: Probabilistic Models for ML
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML"
---

## 01. Title: Probabilistic Models for ML
- Unit scope and role.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Build and compare core probabilistic models.

## 03. Recap from Unit 10
- Generalization and model selection link.

## 04. Why probabilistic modeling
- Represent uncertainty explicitly.

## 05. Generative vs discriminative models
- Modeling p(x,y) vs p(y|x).

## 06. Latent-variable intuition
- Hidden structure in data.

## 07. Directed graphical models
- Bayesian network basics.

## 08. Undirected graphical models
- Markov random field intuition.

## 09. Conditional independence
- Factorization benefits.

## 10. Naive Bayes revisited
- Simple baseline with strong assumptions.

## 11. Gaussian discriminant analysis
- Class-conditional density models.

## 12. Mixture models overview
- Multi-modal data representation.

## 13. EM algorithm intuition
- Alternating latent/parameter updates.

## 14. Gaussian mixture models
- Soft clustering probability view.

## 15. Hidden Markov models (intro)
- Sequence-state modeling.

## 16. Inference tasks
- Marginals, MAP, prediction.

## 17. Exact vs approximate inference
- Tractability tradeoffs.

## 18. Variational inference (preview)
- Optimization perspective.

## 19. Sampling-based inference (preview)
- Monte Carlo perspective.

## 20. Parameter estimation strategies
- MLE, MAP, Bayesian posterior.

## 21. Model evidence and complexity
- Balancing fit and simplicity.

## 22. Information criteria (AIC/BIC)
- Practical model selection tools.

## 23. Calibration in probabilistic models
- Trustworthy uncertainty outputs.

## 24. Proper scoring rules
- NLL and Brier score intuition.

## 25. OOD detection signals
- Density and confidence caveats.

## 26. Failure mode: model misspecification
- Wrong assumptions consequences.

## 27. Failure mode: latent collapse
- Degenerate solutions.

## 28. Failure mode: overconfident posteriors
- Misleading certainty.

## 29. Mitigation checklist
- Diagnostics and regularization.

## 30. Probabilistic view of regularization
- Prior-induced penalties.

## 31. Relation to deep probabilistic models
- Bridge to VAEs/BNNs.

## 32. Sequential data context
- Forecasting and state tracking.

## 33. Missing-data handling
- Marginalization advantage.

## 34. Causal caution
- Probabilistic ≠ causal by default.

## 35. Materials example: property uncertainty
- Predictive distributions in practice.

## 36. Materials example: defect-state HMM
- Temporal process interpretation.

## 37. Materials example: mixture for phases
- Multi-modal structure-property mapping.

## 38. Evaluation protocol
- Accuracy + calibration + likelihood.

## 39. Reproducibility requirements
- Inference settings and seeds.

## 40. Bridge to Unit 12
- Neural networks with probabilistic intuition.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: Naive Bayes baseline
- Train and inspect assumptions.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: fit GMM
- Cluster and evaluate fit.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: EM diagnostics
- Convergence and sensitivity checks.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: calibration analysis
- Reliability assessment.

## 45. Exercise scaffold: model comparison
- AIC/BIC + validation metrics.

## 46. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 47. Summary table
- Models, assumptions, strengths.

## 48. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 49. Reading assignment
- Intro to neural network foundations.

## 50. References
- Source list.

::: {#refs}
:::
